summary: CLI relays user GitHub PAT and deploy hooks to hardcoded author server, then auto-injects mutable remote script into production site
details: When the CLI is run, `bin/setup.js` collects a user-entered GitHub Personal Access Token (with `repo, user:email` scope), a Vercel deploy hook, and an N8N webhook, and POSTs them to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint at `https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/api/settings` (`syncConfigToServer` around bin/setup.js:920, with `masterUrl` set at line 860). README phrasing about 'your AutoHeal dev server' obscures that the destination is fixed to the author's Render instance — the user has no way to direct this to their own server. Whoever controls the Render endpoint receives a full-access GitHub PAT for the user's account. Separately, the CLI writes `<script src="https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/sdk/autoheal.js"></script>` into the user's `index.html` and auto-runs `git add. && git commit && git push` to deploy it (around bin/setup.js:956 and bin/setup.js:1010). The injected script URL has no version pin or SRI hash, so any future change to the served JavaScript executes in every visitor's browser on the user's production site. The README also recommends wiring `autoheal-setup` into a `postinstall` hook, which would cause every contributor's `npm install` to (re)transmit credentials to the same endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
